Definition: Unique identifier of the game player.
Number of matches played.
Number of active days. (i.e., distinct days with at least one activity)
Number of matches with "win" outcomes (there are only two outcomes: wins and losses).
Number of kills recorded, referring to the killing of actual players on the opposing team.
Number of "robot" kills recorded (e.g., minions).
Number of attack activities that ultimately led to kills by other players, where you contributed.
Number of deaths tallied.
Abbreviation for play minutes: Total play minutes recorded.
Gold (in-game currency) compensated, serving as a proxy for in-game performance.
Unique maps played: Number of distinct maps played by this user.
Unique characters played: Number of distinct characters (i.e., heroes) played by this user.
Average matches played per day: Calculated as matches divided by active days.
The greatest number of matches played by the user on a single day.
The greatest number of wins achieved by the user on a single day.
The ratio of matches played during the last week of the calibration period: matches during the last week divided by total matches.
Days passed since the last activity up to the first day of the prediction period.
Days passed since sign-up up to the first day of the prediction period.
Purchase amount in U.S. dollars.
Purchase frequency (i.e., number of purchases).
Indicates whether the user purchased an item that provides access to all game characters, known as a premium feature.
Chat rate per match: Average number of chats typed by the user per match.
Number of minutes taken for the user to play their first match from the sign-up time.
Indicates whether a high-speed fiber internet connection is detected (1) or not (0).
Graphic setting: a = low resolution, b = baseline resolution, c = high resolution, d = super high resolution.
Progress of completed tutorial, where zero means 0% and one means 100%.
Dependent variable: purchase amount in US dollar
Dependent variable: whether the user has churned (one) or not.
